title: Peppery Flaky Ukrainian Beef Dumplings
description: Tender feather blade beef wrapped in a flaky pastry, served with a peppery sauce for a delightful brunch dish. This recipe combines the heartiness of Ukrainian cuisine with the freshness of raw ingredients.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine flour, salt, and cold butter. Use a pastry blender or your fingers to work the butter into the flour until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the beaten eggs to the flour mixture, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
In a separate bowl, mix together the chopped beef, black pepper, parsley, garlic, and a pinch of salt.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the chilled dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Use a round cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles of dough.
Place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center of each dough circle. Fold the dough over the filling, forming a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal.
In a large skillet, heat about 1/2 inch of water over medium heat. When the water is hot, add the dumplings and cover the skillet with a lid.
Steam the dumplings for about 10-12 minutes, or until they are cooked through and the pastry is tender.
While the dumplings are cooking, mix together the sour cream, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt and pepper in a small bowl.
Serve the dumplings hot with the peppery sauce spooned over the top.
